🔬 Science

Energy in Daily Life

Discover the invisible force that powers our world - energy!

📚 Unit 7 ⏱️ 10 min read

Unit 7: Energy in Daily Life

Energy is the ability to do work. Energy makes things happen – it allows us to move, think, see, hear, and live.

What is Energy?

Key PointExplanation
Energy is invisibleWe cannot see energy, but we see its effects
Energy can be transferredMoves from one place to another
Energy can be transformedChanges from one form to another
Energy cannot be created or destroyedTotal energy stays the same (Law of Conservation of Energy)

💡 Did you know? The food you eat contains chemical energy that your body uses to move and grow.

Forms of Energy

Form of EnergyWhat it isExamples
Light EnergyEnergy that allows us to seeSun, light bulb, firefly
Heat Energy (Thermal)Energy that makes things warmFire, heater, sun
Sound EnergyEnergy we hearMusic, talking, bell
Electrical EnergyEnergy from electricityFan, TV, computer
Mechanical EnergyEnergy of moving objectsA moving car, wind, water
Chemical EnergyEnergy stored in substancesFood, battery, fuel
Nuclear EnergyEnergy from atomsSun, nuclear power plant
Solar EnergyEnergy from the sunSolar panels, sunlight

Energy Transformation

Energy can change from one form to another.

TransformationExample
Chemical → Light + HeatA burning candle
Electrical → Light + HeatA light bulb
Electrical → SoundA speaker
Electrical → MechanicalA fan (electricity makes blades move)
Chemical → MechanicalEating food gives you energy to run
Solar → ElectricalSolar panels
Light → ChemicalPhotosynthesis (plants turn light into food)

Energy Transformation Chain Examples

Example 1: Turning on a lamp

Electrical Energy → Light Energy + Heat Energy

Example 2: Playing a guitar

Chemical Energy (from food) → Mechanical Energy (moving fingers) → Sound Energy

Example 3: A car moving

Chemical Energy (fuel) → Heat Energy → Mechanical Energy (wheels move)

Sources of Energy

Renewable Energy Sources (Can be replaced)

SourceHow it worksAdvantagesDisadvantages
SolarSunlight converted to electricityClean, unlimitedOnly works when sunny
WindWind turns turbinesClean, freeNeeds windy places
Hydro (Water)Flowing water turns turbinesClean, reliableDams affect rivers
GeothermalHeat from inside EarthConstant supplyOnly in certain places
BiomassBurning plant/animal wasteUses wasteCan cause air pollution

Non-Renewable Energy Sources (Cannot be replaced)

SourceHow it worksAdvantagesDisadvantages
CoalBurned for heat/electricityCheap, plentifulPollutes, will run out
Oil (Petroleum)Refined into fuelEasy to transportPollutes, expensive
Natural GasBurned for heat/electricityBurns cleaner than coalStill pollutes, will run out

⚠️ Warning: Non-renewable sources are limited. We must use renewable energy to protect the planet.

Light Energy

Properties of Light

PropertyExplanation
Travels in straight linesLight does not bend by itself
Travels very fast300,000 km per second
Can be reflectedBounces off surfaces
Can be refractedBends when passing through different materials
Needs no mediumCan travel through empty space (unlike sound)

Luminous vs. Non-Luminous Objects

TypeMeaningExamples
LuminousProduces its own lightSun, stars, light bulb, firefly
Non-luminousDoes not produce light; reflects lightMoon, table, book, mirror

Transparent, Translucent, Opaque

TypeLight passes throughExamples
TransparentCompletelyGlass, clear water, air
TranslucentPartiallyFrosted glass, butter paper
OpaqueNot at allWood, metal, brick

Reflection of Light

Reflection = light bouncing off a surface.

Type of surfaceReflection
Smooth surface (mirror)Clear reflection (image seen)
Rough surface (wall)Scattered reflection (no clear image)

Refraction of Light

Refraction = light bending when it passes from one material to another.

Examples:

  • A straw looks bent in a glass of water
  • A fish in water looks closer than it really is

Heat Energy

Sources of Heat

SourceExample
SunMain source of heat for Earth
Burning fuelFire, gas stove
ElectricityHeater, iron, toaster
FrictionRubbing hands together
Chemical reactionsHand warmers

Effects of Heat

EffectExplanationExample
ExpansionThings get bigger when heatedGaps in railway tracks
ContractionThings get smaller when cooledThermometer liquid goes down
Change of stateSolid ↔ Liquid ↔ GasIce melts when heated
Increase in temperatureGets hotterWater boils

Heat Transfer

MethodHow it worksExamples
ConductionHeat moves through solid materialsMetal spoon gets hot in tea
ConvectionHeat moves through liquids and gasesHot air rises, cool air sinks
RadiationHeat travels as waves (needs no medium)Heat from the sun

Conductors and Insulators

TypeAllows heat to passExamples
ConductorYesMetals (iron, copper, aluminum)
InsulatorNoWood, plastic, rubber, cloth

💡 Good to know: Cooking pots are made of metal (conductor) but have plastic handles (insulator) so you do not burn your hand.

Sound Energy

How Sound is Produced

Sound is produced when something vibrates (moves back and forth quickly).

SourceWhat vibrates
Human voiceVocal cords
GuitarStrings
DrumSkin (membrane)
BellMetal body

How Sound Travels

Sound needs a medium (solid, liquid, or gas) to travel. Sound cannot travel in empty space (vacuum).

MediumSpeed of sound (approx.)
Air (gas)343 m/s (slowest)
Water (liquid)1,480 m/s
Iron (solid)5,120 m/s (fastest)

💡 Did you know? Sound travels fastest through solids because particles are closer together.

Properties of Sound

PropertyMeaningExample
LoudnessHow strong the sound isA drum is loud, a whisper is soft
PitchHow high or low the sound isA whistle is high pitch, a drum is low pitch

Reflection of Sound (Echo)

Echo = sound bouncing back from a hard surface.

Example: Shouting in a large empty hall or near mountains.

Electrical Energy

What is Electricity?

Electricity is the flow of tiny particles called electrons through a wire.

Simple Electric Circuit

A complete circuit needs four things:

ComponentSymbolFunction
Cell/Battery┤├Provides energy
Wires───Carry electricity
Switch╱╲Turns on/off
Bulb/Load(●)Uses electricity

A complete circuit looks like:

Battery → Wire → Switch → Bulb → Wire → Battery

Conductors and Insulators (Electricity)

TypeAllows electricity to passExamples
ConductorYesCopper, aluminum, iron, water
InsulatorNoRubber, plastic, wood, glass

⚠️ Safety: Never touch electrical wires with wet hands. Water is a conductor!

Uses of Electricity at Home

ApplianceUse
FanCooling (mechanical energy)
Light bulbLight (light energy)
HeaterHeat (heat energy)
TVEntertainment (sound and light)
RefrigeratorKeep food cold

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. Energy is the ability to do ____________.
  2. The main source of light and heat for Earth is the ____________.
  3. Sound is produced by ____________.
  4. Materials that allow electricity to pass through are called ____________.
  5. Materials that do NOT allow heat to pass through are called ____________.

Exercise 2: Identify the Form of Energy

Write the form of energy (Light, Heat, Sound, Electrical, Chemical, Mechanical).

  1. A burning candle gives off ______ and ______.
  2. A moving bicycle has ______ energy.
  3. A battery stores ______ energy.
  4. A ringing bell produces ______ energy.
  5. A fan uses ______ energy.

Exercise 3: Renewable or Non-renewable?

Write “Renewable” or “Non-renewable”.

  1. Coal ______
  2. Solar ______
  3. Wind ______
  4. Oil ______
  5. Water (hydro) ______

Exercise 4: Conductor or Insulator?

Write “Conductor” or “Insulator”.

  1. Copper ______
  2. Rubber ______
  3. Plastic ______
  4. Iron ______
  5. Wood ______

Exercise 5: Match the Energy Transformation

DeviceEnergy transformation
Solar panela) Electrical → Sound
Speakerb) Light → Electrical
Fanc) Electrical → Mechanical
Light bulbd) Electrical → Light + Heat

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. work, 2. sun, 3. vibration, 4. conductors, 5. insulators

Exercise 2: 1. Light, Heat; 2. Mechanical; 3. Chemical; 4. Sound; 5. Electrical

Exercise 3: 1. Non-renewable, 2. Renewable, 3. Renewable, 4. Non-renewable, 5. Renewable

Exercise 4: 1. Conductor, 2. Insulator, 3. Insulator, 4. Conductor, 5. Insulator

Exercise 5: Solar panel→b, Speaker→a, Fan→c, Light bulb→d

Great work! Energy powers everything you do. Use it wisely!
